title: Last Minute Biology EOC Cram Session // 25min Crash Bio Review!
body: Her over here covers my entire school year with 25 mins, yet she teaches better than my bio teacher 😭, not reading off of the notes or doing any fill in the blank stuff. Anyone else here the day before the test, or taking the STAAR EOC tmrw?
author: @VinaySprints

title: Last Minute Biology EOC Cram Session // 25min Crash Bio Review!
body:
Heres the AI version of my message😭

I took my Florida EOC today and scored a 5. There were 51 questions on my exam. Here's an overview of the topics covered:

Few questions on Embryology
Many questions on ecology (environment and ecosystems)
1 or 2 questions on energy in ecosystems/energy pyramid
3 or 4 questions on organelles
1 or 2 questions on parts of the brain
1 question on viscosity of blood
Many questions on cells & cell theory
Few questions on cellular respiration and photosynthesis
2 or 3 questions on monomers, polymers & macromolecules (like lipids, amino acids, monosaccharides, carbohydrates, proteins, glucose, starch)
1 question on identifying a macromolecule, monomer, or polymer based on an image
Many questions on evolution
1 question on Punnett squares
2 or 3 questions on genetics & inheritance (other than Punnett square question)
1 or 2 questions on Taxonomy, classification, & domains
1 or 2 questions on codons in relation to DNA & RNA
2 or 3 questions on natural selection & other biological processes
1 question on a food web with a diagram
3 or 4 questions on understanding scientific theories and laws
1 question on microscopes (specifically viewing cells via microscopes)
2 or 3 questions on the carbon cycle & sustainability
Few questions on plants (reproduction, genetics, etc)
1 question on properties of water
Some questions were common sense. It's important to eliminate answers you know are wrong and to read the entire question and answers carefully. Words like "most likely" or "best" in relation to the answer choices may indicate that the answer is not 100% correct but is the most plausible based on scientific principles.

When answering questions, even if you feel like you totally understand it, find key words and define them.

- Mitosis, for example, is a type of cell division that creates body cells in animals (including humans) and is involved in growth, tissue repair, and asexual reproduction in some organisms. It creates two identical diploid cells with the exact same DNA as the parent cell. During mitosis, cells spend most of their time preparing for division and less time actually dividing. The process involves the stages of prophase, metaphase, anaphase, and telophase, followed by cytokinesis;

- Fungi are heterotrophic organisms that have cytoplasm, cell walls, and are eukaryotic. They are decomposers, breaking down organic matter;

- Bacteria can be autotrophic or heterotrophic and most have cell walls. They are prokaryotic, unicellular organisms with ribosomes. Mutations in bacteria can help them develop immunity/resistance against antibiotics, similar to how animals evolve through evolution.

It's important to understand the relationships between concepts in the questions and how to interpret diagrams based on that. A testing strategy that helped me was to eliminate choices I knew were wrong and select the best answer, flagging the question to come back to it later. Going back and double-checking can help clarify any questions you struggled with. MOST IMPORTANTLY READ THE QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS CAEFULLY TO AVOID OVERLOOKING IMPORTANT INFORMATION OR WORDS THAT SWIFT THE MEANING OF THE STATEMENT!

I hope this helps! Good luck to everyone taking the exam.❤
author: @AveryUsoro

title: UNC CHAPEL HILL vs NC STATE UNIVERSITY -WHICH IS BETTER? (comparing campuses, dorms, transport, etc)
body: Hey y’all! this vid has been getting a decent amount of views recently so I just wanted to say that yes, my opinion has slightly changed on a few of these topics over the past year after seeing them both with minimal Covid restrictions again. but for the most part, I think most of what I said still stands true. I also have some more experience with some factors that I didn’t mention (ie off campus housing) now, so if you have any questions about anything, feel free to ask and I’ll respond with my updated thoughts!!
author: @ShivNC

title: UNC CHAPEL HILL vs NC STATE UNIVERSITY -WHICH IS BETTER? (comparing campuses, dorms, transport, etc)
body:
In the 1960s, the state of NC did a very smart thing by assigning major programs of study to the two schools so that that they wouldn't be in competition with each other for funding. This has allowed both universities to pursue their own paths to excellence. I think it's important to understand that NC State is North Carolina's original land grant university with its mission responding to the industrial revolution. This shaped its future toward more practical degree programs compared to classical liberal arts.
Also, NC State wins for ice cream. Howling Cow is legit and it's as fresh and locally sourced as you are going to get. The cows are just a few miles south of campus on a research "farm".
author: @jlpack62

title: UNC CHAPEL HILL vs NC STATE UNIVERSITY -WHICH IS BETTER? (comparing campuses, dorms, transport, etc)
body: One should apply to a department not a school. UNC has some better departments and NCSU has some that are better or different - usually more applied or specialized in Raleigh. I applied for UNC Botany (from a Cornell B.S.) only to realize later NCSU"s Top 5 Horticultural Science Dept. was a better fit for my goals. Both schools partner in some areas like Biomedical Engineering. Also talk to profs who may become your advisors. If you find 2-3 profs in a department that align with your interests and goals, that is probably a good fit. If you can't even find one named, world-class prof then go elsewhere.
author: @LarryHatch

author: @colebeans3145

title: UNC CHAPEL HILL vs NC STATE UNIVERSITY -WHICH IS BETTER? (comparing campuses, dorms, transport, etc)
body:
Hi! This is such a great video! I am extremely torn between the two and if you could answer some of my questions that would be so helpful! 1. I thought NCSU was better for business (I want to do marketing) because of their many offered programs, Raleigh’s opportunities, and entrepreneurship support. Plus, as I understand, UNC has very few undergrad business degrees, and if you don’t get into Kenyan Flagler your sophomore year, than is it worth risking that as a business major? 2. You might not have experienced this if you graduated before end of 2020 and then all through 2021, but I’ve heard, and read, about many cases of UNC having a toxic environment regarding respecting people’s political opinions…. This is something few talk about but it’s been a big factor in my decision, because it seems to be that UNC’s both students and definitely professors, can be quite stern and biased regarding reading or hearing opposing views… even failing students who write their honest opinions… 3. How important is the prestige at UNC if you want to go the entrepreneurial route, meaning it won’t be a huge factor having UNC on your resume?
Hope you can answer my questions! Thank you.
author: @tayamishin

author: @johnchetcuti9960

title: What THEY'RE NOT TELLING YOU About Moving To Florida | Honest Review 5 Years Later
body:
I been driving in a large chaotic city on Sweden and came to Miami and I say driving in Miami is so much easier than in Sweden and I never experienced any problems at all. Drive safe and sober then no problem and be aware what others do instead of looking at your phone.
Traffic in Florida is not that hard
author: @sebnature

title: What THEY'RE NOT TELLING YOU About Moving To Florida | Honest Review 5 Years Later
body: I’ve lived in the Tampa Bay Area for almost 20 years, purchased our home in 2015. Our home owners and auto insurance have tripled, every year we get increases. Our property taxes have doubled. I love how you described our area in almost all points but the reality is that our state is degrading and residents are being priced out of paradise. Track home communities are destroying wetlands and the wooded areas, pushing all wildlife out. The community we once had is no longer due to all of the growth you stated.
author: @oneofakindjosh79

title: What THEY'RE NOT TELLING YOU About Moving To Florida | Honest Review 5 Years Later
body: Really appreciate this video I have seen so many where they clearly want to scare you into moving somewhere else.  I just want honesty I think you covered everything really well here
author: @PacketRustler

title: What THEY'RE NOT TELLING YOU About Moving To Florida | Honest Review 5 Years Later
body: I don't believe he is minimizing at all. He is being honest and not over dramatizing. Thanks for being straight about it all.  I've been coming here since I was a kid and my mother has lived here for a very long time. We have seen it change. We had the benefit of her experience before we chose our place.
author: @lisaturkovich8263

title: What THEY'RE NOT TELLING YOU About Moving To Florida | Honest Review 5 Years Later
body: My grandparents moved to Florida in 1990 and half of my family followed. They moved to palm coast and it was tiny back then. Unfortunately, it’s become quite different and not so nice. I live in nj and hate cold; it literally makes me ill. I’ve always dreamed of living in Florida!

title: What THEY'RE NOT TELLING YOU About Moving To Florida | Honest Review 5 Years Later
body: I lived in the 90s. I remember hearing on the radio while I was driving, thousands move to Florida and thousands move out, as well. It's always been a very transitional state.
author: @LimitlessThinker

title: What THEY'RE NOT TELLING YOU About Moving To Florida | Honest Review 5 Years Later
body: I lived in Florida for over 50 years, until one night  in November I took the dog out at 11pm and the weatherman said it was 90 degrees and the humidity was 100% . That's when I decided it was enough and I decided to move North!  I don't regret it. It is like walking around in  a sauna!
author: @AuntieM78

title: What THEY'RE NOT TELLING YOU About Moving To Florida | Honest Review 5 Years Later
body:
From what I’ve gathered after a few videos…
-don’t live right on the coast but don’t live too far inland either, closer to the panhandle if possible(coast is pricey and lots of people, too far inland is swampy, too far down into Florida gets hotter and hotter)
-waterfront isn’t worth it, consider the driving distance to the beach instead
-higher elevation the better to avoid water/storm damage
author: @taylorweems3746

title: What THEY'RE NOT TELLING YOU About Moving To Florida | Honest Review 5 Years Later
body: Super video and lots of help information. I love the fact that he addressed many topics that one might consider taboo about Florida (heat, humidity, rain, bugs, rodents, etc). He addressed these issues in a fair and open way, and I love that. One cute thing that I noticed is the number of times he said "y'all" in this video. At first I found it surprising that a guy from Detroit would say the word "y'all" about every minute and a half, but perhaps he should consider adding to his next "What THEY'RE NOT TELLING YOU About Moving To Florida" videos is that you might start saying "y'all" after living there for just a few years. All the best.
author: @kqschwarz

title: What THEY'RE NOT TELLING YOU About Moving To Florida | Honest Review 5 Years Later
body: Our house is on the market now. Hoping to be there by early spring! Your video was very informative! We’re completely ready for all of it! Get ready… here we come!
author: @gms2606

title: Honest Review of Beech Mountain Ski Resort in North Carolina

author: @basketballca-c1d

title: Honest Review of Beech Mountain Ski Resort in North Carolina
body: I haven't been skiing at Beech since 2014, but I always enjoyed it better than some of the other mountains in western NC! However, I have to say that I was rather disappointed that it didn't replace its 1987 Doppelmayr high-speed quad lift with another high-speed lift. (I think they wanted to update both that lift AND the old black diamond lift instead of buying just one really nice lift.) On the other hand, I'm glad the Lower Shawneehaw lift, seen at 1:30, was replaced with a quad lift even if it's not a high-speed lift. The original dual-chair lift was SOOOO sloowww, especially whenever ski school students would fall down while getting off at the top; plus, it could only carry half as many people as the new one can.
author: @CJWJR

title: Honest Review of Beech Mountain Ski Resort in North Carolina
body: Hard  to beat midweek skiing in NC, usually no one on the mountain and $40ish for lift tickets at most spots. You can rip it pretty good for half a day while everyone else is at work.
author: @TeKnoVKNG23

title: Honest Review of Beech Mountain Ski Resort in North Carolina
body:
Beech has a mountain top bar and their own Brewery on the mountain! Thats pretty unique if you ask me. Also, there are plenty of "Glade" runs in the woods if you know where to look.
Beech is more of a resort feel whereas Sugar has more terrain.
author: @jonahwomble58

title: Honest Review of Beech Mountain Ski Resort in North Carolina
body: I tried skiing here (and at Sugar) once, thinking it’s a lot closer to where I live and so travel would be a lot easier than Colorado (so I could add an extra ski trip that season).  Perfectly suitable but it lacks the one thing that a large, western resort has and that is the best part of skiing - areas that are too far and scary for beginners.  Every run, regardless of rating, is crowded with beginners who don’t really understand or don’t yet know how to ride without being hazards to other riders.  Everyone was a beginner at some point, but there are no back bowls or areas that beginners won’t visit so you spend the day dodging out of control riders and getting mowed down.  Obvious upside is that you don’t have hour-long lift lines at choke points like you do out west - a new and really quite significant issue lately at Vail, Breck, etc.
author: @philipmarsh3522

title: Honest Review of Beech Mountain Ski Resort in North Carolina
body: are those prices during the rush season around Christmas? I’ve got a trip planned for next Christmas there and I’m comparing prices to previous ski resorts I’ve been to.
author: @lucasdowning9774

title: Honest Review of Beech Mountain Ski Resort in North Carolina
body: So im driving from raleigh..idk why i wanted to ask this but is it safe to drive up there or do i need a SUV?  THANKS FOR PUTTING UP THE PRICES.  Its my first time so im excited. Its 3.5 hours away from me is a 12 hr sesh good or 8? & hows the night rides
author: @jerseyltd

title: Honest Review of Beech Mountain Ski Resort in North Carolina
body:
Ive been to both Sugar and Beech for over 10 years. Beech is light years better than Sugar in almost every single aspect. The runs are wider and have better snow. The facilities are nicer and are upgraded more frequently. Most of the 4-pack lifts are high speed. The ones at Sugar haven’t been updated in ages. There’s no giant eyesore at the top of the mountain, instead they have an awesome summit bar (should’ve mentioned it).

After I went to Beech once, I’ve never wanted to go back to Sugar. I would only even consider going if Beech was closed or somehow packed and Sugar wasn’t, but even that is super unlikely. If Beech is crowded, Sugar is usually a nightmare.
author: @nickruiz3996

title: Honest Review of Beech Mountain Ski Resort in North Carolina
body: i really like doing a warm up run on that oz back slope and i also love the history of the mountain and the theme park which resides at the summit
author: @MJtoast

title: Honest Review of Beech Mountain Ski Resort in North Carolina
body: Beech is great in the early or late season. In the busy season it’s ridiculously bad. Unorganized and chaos. Late December-January I’m going to Sugar or App. Beech is a no go unless I’m doing a night session
author: @camcambambam5128

title: Honest Review of Beech Mountain Ski Resort in North Carolina
body: I was at beech for 2 days (Friday and Saturday) in mid February 2025 and it was AWFUL.  Insanely crowded. Funneling all those people into a tight area to get on lifts leads to massive congestion issues. It also mingles the beginner trails with faster advanced riders in some pretty narrow areas. Lift crews did nothing to maintain chairlift off ramps (solid ice) or keep them clear of new skiers who’d fall and block them. Having gone to school nearby in Boone I’ve ridden this mountain for 20 years and absolutely despise this mountain on weekends. The flex ticketing is really nice but don’t waste your money here.
author: @planetxmetal

title: Honest Review of Beech Mountain Ski Resort in North Carolina
body: The most unique thing about Beech is that it used to have the "Land of Oz" theme park on the backside of the mountain.  Although it is now only open one weekend a year, it's still THE most unique thing about it.  Property on and near Beech has been much more reasonably priced than other NC ski resorts.  We would regularly drive past Sugar just to get to Beech pretty much EVERY time when I was younger (in the 80's)
author: @n-da-bunka2650

title: The Unofficial NC State Complete Campus Tour - Main and Centennial
body:
A few places I didn’t get to in the video that I think are important:

-Frat Court: pretty self explanatory, a little bit off campus on Western Blvd/Varsity Dr. Looks really nice

-SAS Hall/Park Shops: class buildings on Stinson Dr (underneath the Court of NC), mainly for statistics

-Wolf Village: student apartments that freshmen are allowed to stay in, usually 4bed/2bath units. On the very west of campus.

-PNC Arena/Carter Finley Stadium: obviously where football and basketball teams play. About a 10 minute drive from campus on Trinity Rd
author: @ShivNC

title: The Unofficial NC State Complete Campus Tour - Main and Centennial
body: It's funny to think that my old dorm is now one of the honors dorms. I just lived there because it was close to the Design School.
author: @jpack85

title: The Unofficial NC State Complete Campus Tour - Main and Centennial
body: Awesome, enjoyed it. Fitts-Woolard Hall has a nice backstory about it's donors. Ed Wollard (an NCSU grad) was Chairman of the Board at Apple when they were failing. He decided he needed to save the company. and was looking for the right CEO. He put on his hat of contrition and called up Steve Jobs and begged him to come back. Jobs had some terms like fire most everyone. Wollard got the Board to do that and the rest is iPhone history. Had Woolard not made that phone call and got the Board to go along with Job's demands....
author: @LarryHatch

title: The Unofficial NC State Complete Campus Tour - Main and Centennial
body:
🎯 Key points for quick navigation:

00:23 NC State's campus on Hillsboro Street features student apartments like Valentine Commons and Nelson Hall, home to the Poole College of Management.
01:19 Surrounding the grass field are science-related buildings like Fox and David Clarke, primarily housing labs and research facilities.
02:46 The Brickyard is a central campus area adjacent to Hill Library, a key spot for studying and dining, including options like Chick-fil-A and pizza.
06:06 The Court of North Carolina is a scenic grass field surrounded by academic buildings like Poe, Harrelson, and Caldwell Hall.
09:22 Tally Student Union and Carmichael Gym are major hubs on campus offering dining, recreational facilities, and student services.
15:33 Centennial Campus hosts engineering and textile facilities, including buildings like Hunt Library and Fitzwillard Hall, known for advanced study spaces and labs.
20:37 Main library at NC State emphasizes automated book retrieval over traditional stacks.
21:08 Textiles complex houses key resources for popular textiles major, including classrooms and a scenic view.
21:48 MRC building serves as a major research hub for professors and grad students at NC State.

Made with HARPA AI

title: Day in the Life - Police Officer
body:
Hi everyone! I would like to thank you all for watching the documentary! I did not think it would reach people throughout the country and throughout the world so fast. I’m forever grateful for the opportunity I had to be part of this and I am happy to see all the positivity.

I’d also like to thank Timur and Jorge for making this documentary! They are amazing at what they do and it would not have been possible without them.
And yes… I still love matcha and Jeeps lol

“In the end, it's not the years in your life that count. It's the life in your years.” - Abraham Lincoln
author: @Zentfo94

title: Day in the Life - Police Officer
body: Respect to this guy and his team of officers, you can tell he's super passionate, friendly and reasonable with people. We need more officers like him, I hope he rises in the ranks and becomes an influence for other officers.

title: Day in the Life - Police Officer

title: Basic Training Day in the Life
body:
I leave for basic in 6 days, although I'm pretty nervous I can't wait to experience it & become a better me.
(Update 1): Finally graduated BCT a few days ago, I am now in Arizona for AIT.

(Update 2): Done with AIT. Graduated damn near top of my class. Off to Airborne School.

(Update 3): Finished airborne a while ago, now to wait out my contract lol. It’s all relatively easy in retrospect, just do the right shit at the right time in the right uniform.

(Update 4): I have maybe like, 18 months until the end of my contract? Roughly? Time flies.

(Update 5:) One year left, in the TAP (Transition Assistance Program) process as of current. (or so I thought)

(Update 6:) Forgot about this comment for a while, but I just reenlisted for 4 in Hawaii. Probably gonna stay in for a few contracts tbh. (This was June of 2025 roughly, I think.)

(Update 7, since a lot of you wanted some more. Guess I gotta keep this going as a little journal for you guys or something) Currently February 11th, 2026, I shipped out for basic on January 24th, 2023. I'm on my second contract in Hawaii now, I love it here aside from all the traffic. Told myself and everyone else I wasn't gonna do a second but here I am. Have my SGT promotion board next week, got about a week and a half notice about it so I've been studying my ass off. I'll probably forget about this comment for awhile, so, see you guys next update.
author: @SampsonWrestling

title: Basic Training Day in the Life
body: My son went to basic training 3 days out of highschool. Spent a total of almost 17 years in. Couldbt be more proud of him and the army as a whole unit

title: Basic Training Day in the Life
body: I leave to basic in about a month and a half. Never been more excited to get my ass beat into shape in my whole life. I haven’t done shit with myself since I graduated high school 7 years ago. I entered the workforce right after graduating, Worst mistake of my life. Now i plan on correcting the mistake. I’m finally going to make my best effort to be the best I can be. 11x Airborne Infantry.

title: Basic Training Day in the Life
body: My youngest is two weeks in she left one week after graduation. I was so nervous  at first. I would say I'm the most proudest mother ever. From these  two weeks , I can hear the growth , confidence, and structure  that it has given her already. I am very grateful for her decision 🙏🏾 💕

author: @KaePriceMusic

title: Basic Training Day in the Life
body: I went to basic in 1979, scared to death. I was in the last OSUT unit at Ft Gordon. I went from being a civilian  executive secretary to the Distinguished Honor graduate! I proudly wear my veteran t shirts. Best part: when I came home and saw my dad's face! And then watching him trying to pick up his 5'3" soldier daughter's full duffle bag!!❤. Proud to be a US Army Veteran! ❤️🇺🇸 Keep it strong!
author: @reppi8742

title: Basic Training Day in the Life
body:
Very glad for their dedication to join the military family, striving together, training together, and having each other's back no matter what . Best family ever.
Shaves the scalp, exercises , pushes limits , goes through obstacles, goes through the gas chamber, takes apart artillery , and learns to shoot , Marches together, and strives as a family.
Very glad for their oppertunities, stay strong warriors !

author: @natabenicio4958

title: Touring Every Florida College So You Don't Have To | (UF UM UCF FAU ETC)
body: As a high school senior, who is currently looking Florida colleges, I toured UF and wasn’t as impressed by it as a lot of people are. The campus wasn’t really my vibe and everything felt very serious. I actually much preferred USF and UCF campuses and environments so much so that I didn’t end up applying to UF, but it was really interesting to see your takes on the campuses!
author: @bubbleowl1539

title: Touring Every Florida College So You Don't Have To | (UF UM UCF FAU ETC)
body: You should do a video on the social lives of each school (party scene, campus life, etc). You could also add in Lynn and Eckerd!
author: @willasullivan8222

title: Touring Every Florida College So You Don't Have To | (UF UM UCF FAU ETC)
body: Love being an Owl alumni! It’s a completely different campus than when I was there, there was no engineering building, they hadn’t built the gym and new pool until my senior year, and we still had to drive to some random high school stadium 30 minutes away because the stadium hadn’t been built. The rapid development of that campus has been amazing to watch, and I love getting tickets that have access to the full service bar IN THE STADIUM! How awesome is that?!
author: @Alex-cz4fk

title: North Carolina State University Campus Tour
body:
Great job on video!  Brings back tons of fond memories.....the campus has changed a little bit for better I think. 

It's just amazing to see how the Centennial Campus has taken off and been developed since I left. 

Go Pack!!!

title: North Carolina State University Campus Tour
body: My GPA was a 4.0 unweighted and a 4.5 weighted, and my SATs were 1790.  My boyfriends was a 3.5 and his SATs were 1650, so it varies! BTW this video was a project for my Ambassadors class at NCSU and we do campus tours. Everyone should come and take a tour of the campus and see it for yourselves!

title: North Carolina State University Campus Tour
body: FYI Raleigh is not the largest city in North Carolina! Please correct this ASAP. I spent 5 years in our state capitol and was taught to be be technically correct. This statement is an embarrassment to our recruitment process. Would anyone want to go to any school that has incorrect facts on its recruiting video???
author: @kennyrhyne

title: North Carolina State University - Official College Video Tour
body: 📧 If you are working for the school and/or think this school needs updating, please reach out to us through email.
